title: Observability, Service Levels, Support, and Incident Learning · Decision Retrieval
summary: A delayed review reconstructs observability, service levels, support, and incident learning from stakeholder, decision, assumption, revision, artifact, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, owner, and residual-risk evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for user-centered operations and incident system after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose user-centered availability, correctness, latency, freshness, durability, or support indicators with valid measurement boundaries.
Set objective, window, population, exclusions, error budget, and response policy from user need and operating capacity.
Correlate revision, request, actor class, workflow, dependency, event, log, metric, and trace without high-cardinality or privacy leakage.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Incident Learning
steps.instruction:
review case — Many logs report healthy servers while users cannot complete the task and support cannot correlate the failure with actor, workflow, dependency, or revision. The review variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Preserve timeline and evidence, restore safely, communicate impact, analyze contributing conditions without blame, and track preventive work.

Inspect the Incident Learning trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Incident Learning.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for preserve timeline and evidence, restore safely, communicate impact, analyze contributing conditions without blame, and track preventive work. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Incident Learning, keep Incident Learning connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Incident Learning, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Incident Learning, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Incident Learning precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Separate evidence from confidence · Review Incident Learning, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: Many logs report healthy servers while users cannot complete the task and support cannot correlate the failure with actor, workflow, dependency, or revision. The review variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Preserve timeline and evidence, restore safely, communicate impact, analyze contributing conditions without blame, and track preventive work.

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Incident Learning, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: The person who made the triggering change is the incident root cause.

stimulus.title: Incident Learning case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Many logs report healthy servers while users cannot complete the task and support cannot correlate the failure with actor, workflow, dependency, or revision. The review variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Preserve timeline and evidence, restore safely, communicate impact, analyze contributing conditions without blame, and track preventive work.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: The person who made the triggering change is the incident root cause.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve timeline and evidence, restore safely, communicate impact, analyze contributing conditions without blame, and track preventive work. Artifact under review: user-centered operations and incident system.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Incident Learning, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Incident Learning
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Incident Learning: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Many logs report healthy servers while users cannot complete the task and support cannot correlate the failure with actor, workflow, dependency, or revision. The review variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Preserve timeline and evidence, restore safely, communicate impact, analyze contributing conditions without blame, and track preventive work.

Evidence target: Record what required effort · Review Incident Learning.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Record what required effort · Review Incident Learning, keep Incident Learning connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Preserve timeline and evidence, restore safely, communicate impact, analyze contributing conditions without blame, and track preventive work.

hints: For Record what required effort · Review Incident Learning, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Incident Learning precondition, or invariant that must hold.
